name: pool-mcp
description: stdio MCP server exposing pool hosts as Claude Code MCP tools. Two tools: `mcp__pool__on` (run `pool on <host> <command>` and return {stdout, stderr, exit_code, non_claude_load}) and `mcp__pool__list` (read ~/.pool/pool.json and return the roster, pi5-akida excluded by default per sidecar @D s8 unless `include_anima=true`). JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio, implemented in hexa-lang (`_pool_mcp.hexa`). Server registered via the `mcpServers` field; the launcher is a shell read-loop that feeds one JSON-RPC line per `hexa run` invocation (stateless per-message dispatch — keeps the stdio handshake responsive without a long-lived in-process line reader). POC scope — fan-out parallel dispatch / per-tool timeouts deferred to 0.2.0.

args: H="$HEXA"; [ -x "$H" ] || H="$HOME/.hx/bin/hexa"; [ -x "$H" ] || H=$(command -v hexa) || exit 1; while IFS= read -r line; do printf "%s\n" "$line" | "$H" run "$@"; done
